cost_sensitivity.cost_model: Future diagnostics must reject interpretation if any candidate relationship only survives before fees, slippage, and spread assumptions.
cost_sensitivity.scope: Value-free migration metadata only; no execution cost estimate is produced.

cost_assumptions.scope: research planning only; no execution or tradability claim

data_assumptions.data_dependency_detail: CPI scheduled release dates/times are public and small to encode; CPI surprise/actual values require an external economic-data feed. No economic-calendar source exists in the repo. External macro feed deferred.

exclusion_rules: Scheduled release time carried as known-ahead point-in-time metadata (_require_metadata_known_as_of guard). Surprise magnitude is used only as an OFFLINE diagnostic split / labels-only conditioning, never wired into a live (causal) feature contract.
Do not test needs_paid_data records in V0; paid-data sourcing is RED-deferred.
Do not edit or delete the legacy Track-A card until a later retirement phase is authorized.
expected_failure_modes: Conditioning on a scheduled-release clock is structurally distinct from realized-price-action and BBO-state conditioning used by the rejected mechanisms. The reversion-vs-continuation OUTCOME may overlap with vwap_session/sweep dynamics, so the orthogonality claim is weaker than for fomc_drift and must be checked, not assumed.

Residualize forward outcomes against the vwap_session and regime dead-mechanism factors before scoring the CPI-window conditioner; report the conditioner's marginal contribution and an explicit overlap diagnostic.

CPI scheduled release dates/times are public and small to encode; CPI surprise/actual values require an external economic-data feed. No economic-calendar source exists in the repo. External macro feed deferred.
promotion_criteria: Any future WATCH or CANDIDATE routing requires reviewer-gated evidence and must preserve EXPLORATORY promotion guards.
Any future diagnostic must pass no-lookahead, duplicate-exposure, and testability checks before interpretation.

timestamp_assumptions.lookahead_description: CPI SURPRISE (actual vs consensus) is the natural conditioner but is knowable ONLY after the release. Using it as a live feature would be lookahead. Scheduled release time is known ahead and is safe.
timestamp_assumptions.lookahead_level: high lookahead risk declared by the legacy Track-A card

hypothesis_card.author: ALPHA_IDEA_TO_VERDICT_LOOP_V0/IVL-P01
hypothesis_card.expected_mechanism: Around scheduled CPI releases, we test whether forward intraday behavior conditioned on the post-release window (and, as an offline-only split, on surprise magnitude) shows a measurable reversion-versus-continuation pattern relative to non-event sessions. Falsifiable conditioning hypothesis; not a profitability claim.
falsification_criteria: Reject or requeue this migrated record if required governed features or labels are unavailable before diagnostics.
Block any later verdict if no-lookahead controls or duplicate exposure checks fail before a metric is inspected.
hypothesis_card.rationale: Scheduled inflation-print information arrival can trigger an initial impulse followed by liquidity-provision dynamics; whether the post-impulse path reverts or continues is an empirical question the diagnostics resolve.
risks: CPI SURPRISE (actual vs consensus) is the natural conditioner but is knowable ONLY after the release. Using it as a live feature would be lookahead. Scheduled release time is known ahead and is safe.

Conditioning on a scheduled-release clock is structurally distinct from realized-price-action and BBO-state conditioning used by the rejected mechanisms. The reversion-vs-continuation OUTCOME may overlap with vwap_session/sweep dynamics, so the orthogonality claim is weaker than for fomc_drift and must be checked, not assumed.
